---
title: "policy_learning_nitrate_regional_test"
author: "Xindi Lin"
date: "2025-10-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(ranger)
library(SuperLearner)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(WeightIt)
library(GpGp)
library(Metrics)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(ggplot2)
library(see)
library(yardstick)
library(xgboost)
library(glmnet)
library(kernlab)
library(glmnet)
library(numDeriv)
library(MASS)
library(e1071)
library(WeightIt)
```

```{r}
source('data_generation.R')
source('funcs_kriging.R')
source('loss_functions.R')
source('loss_functions_RKHS.R')
```

#### load regional data and preprocess

```{r}
file_path <- "/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/data_Nitrate_with_covar_median_well.csv"
## we will use the one with the proportion
# file_path <- "/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/data_Nitrate_latest_with_covar_landuseProp_median_well"
command <- paste("brctl download", shQuote(file_path))
system(command)
data <- read.csv("/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/data_Nitrate_with_covar_median_well.csv")
```

remove zero-inflated

```{r}
data <- data[data$concentration_plus_median!=0.5,]
```

choose area

```{r}
data$area = ""

indices_central <- data$County %in% c("Wood", "Portage", "Waupaca", "Juneau", "Adams", "Waushara", "Marquette", "Green Lake")
data[indices_central, "area"] = "Central"

indices_westCentral <- data$County %in% c("Buffalo", "Trempealeau", "La Crosse", "Monroe")
data[indices_westCentral, "area"] = "West Central"

indices_southCentral <- data$County %in% c("Columbia", "Dodge", "Dane", "Jefferson", "Green", "Rock")
data[indices_southCentral, "area"] = "South Central"

indices_eastCentral <- data$County %in% c("Outagamie", "Winnebago", "Door", "Fond du Lac", "Brown", "Calumet","Sheboygan","Manitowoc","Kewaunee")
data[indices_eastCentral, "area"] = "East Central"

indices_southWest <- data$County %in% c("Vernon", "Crawford", "Grant", "Richland", "Sauk", "Iowa","Lafayette")
data[indices_southWest, "area"] = "South West"

indices_southEast <- data$County %in% c("Washington", "Waukesha", "Walworth", "Ozaukee", "Milwaukee", "Racine","Kenosha")
data[indices_southEast, "area"] = "South East"
```

```{r}
data <- data[data$area == "East Central",]
```


```{r}
set.seed(1998)

data_full <- data
# train_indices <- sample(nrow(data_full), nrow(data_full) * 0.7) # we would replace this with a stratified sample splitting
# we partition to balance the distribution of nitrate
train_indices <- createDataPartition(data$logconcentration_plus_median, list = FALSE, p = 0.7) # from caret package
data <- data_full[train_indices,]
data_test <- data_full[-train_indices,]
```

add log transformation

```{r}
data$logWellDepth = log(data$WellDepth)
data$logconcentration_plus_median = log(data$concentration_plus_median)
data_test$logWellDepth = log(data_test$WellDepth)
data_test$logconcentration_plus_median = log(data_test$concentration_plus_median)
```

#### Estimate nuisance parameter

##### OR RKHS

```{r}
OR_covariate_names <- c("logWellDepth","WellUse","crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog","StaticLevel")
```

```{r}
design_matrix <- model.matrix(~ . , data = data[, OR_covariate_names])[,-1]
design_matrix_test <- model.matrix(~ . , data = data_test[, OR_covariate_names])[,-1]
```

```{r}
svm_auto <- svm(x = design_matrix, y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, type='nu-regression')
```

```{r}
gpfit_RKHS = GpGp::fit_model(svm_auto$residuals,
                        locs = data[,c("longitude", "latitude")],
                        covfun_name = "matern_sphere")

krige_values_RKHS <- leave_one_out_kriging(locs = data[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                                      y_obs = svm_auto$residuals, 
                                      gp_model = gpfit_RKHS$covfun_name,
                                      gp_params = gpfit_RKHS$covparms,
                                      order = c("coordinate")) 

krige_values_test_RKHS <- GpGp::predictions(fit = gpfit_RKHS, 
                      locs_pred = data_test[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                      X_pred = rep(1,nrow(data_test)))
```

```{r}
cat('Test rMSE: with kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median), predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix_test) + krige_values_test_RKHS))
cat('\n')
cat('Test rMSE: No kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median), predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix_test)))
cat('\n')

cat('Training rMSE: with kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data$concentration_plus_median), predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix) + krige_values_RKHS))
cat('\n')
cat('Training rMSE: No kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data$concentration_plus_median), predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix)))
cat('\n')
```

##### OR XGBoost

```{r}
OR_covariate_names <- c("logWellDepth","WellUse","crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog","StaticLevel")
```

```{r}
design_matrix <- model.matrix(~ . , data = data[, OR_covariate_names])[,-1]
design_matrix_test <- model.matrix(~ . , data = data_test[, OR_covariate_names])[,-1]
```

We might do cross-validation 

```{r}
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = design_matrix, label = data$logconcentration_plus_median)

params <- list(
  objective = "reg:squarederror", # Specify regression task
  eta = 0.1,                      # Learning rate
  max_depth = 4,                  # Maximum depth of a tree
  monotone_constraints = c(0, -1, rep(0,15)), # adding this actually improve the performance
  eval_metric = "rmse"            # The metric to evaluate
)

cv_results <- xgb.cv(
  params = params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = 500,
  nfold = 5,
  early_stopping_rounds = 50, # Stop if performance doesn't improve
  verbose = 0                 # Suppress verbose output
)
```

```{r}
set.seed(1998)
# 4. Evaluate the results
print("Cross-validation results (last 5 rounds):")
# print(tail(cv_results$evaluation_log))
print((cv_results$evaluation_log))

# Find the best number of rounds based on the lowest test RMSE
best_iteration <- cv_results$best_iteration
print(paste("Best iteration:", best_iteration))
```


```{r}
OR_mean_model_xgboost <- xgboost(data = design_matrix, 
                                 label = data$logconcentration_plus_median,
                                 eta = 0.1,                      # Learning rate
                                 max_depth = 4, 
                                 # label = data$concentration_plus_median,
                                 nrounds = cv_results$best_iteration,
                                 monotone_constraints = c(0, -1, rep(0,15)))
```

```{r}
importance_matrix <- xgb.importance(feature_names = colnames(design_matrix), model = OR_mean_model_xgboost)
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix = importance_matrix)
```

```{r}
Metrics::rmse(data$logconcentration_plus_median, predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost,design_matrix))
Metrics::rmse(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median), predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost,design_matrix_test))
```

```{r}
set.seed(1998)
loocv_model <- xgb.cv(
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = cv_results$best_iteration,
  eta = 0.1,                      # Learning rate
  max_depth = 4, 
  monotone_constraints = c(0, -1, rep(0,15)),
  nfold = 10,                 # Key step for LOOCV
  prediction = TRUE,         # Important: tells the function to return predictions
  verbose = 0                # Suppress progress messages
)
```

```{r}
gpfit = GpGp::fit_model(data$logconcentration_plus_median - loocv_model$pred,
                        locs = data[,c("longitude", "latitude")],
                        covfun_name = "matern_sphere")

krige_values <- leave_one_out_kriging(locs = data[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                                      y_obs = data$logconcentration_plus_median - loocv_model$pred, 
                                      gp_model = gpfit$covfun_name,
                                      gp_params = gpfit$covparms,
                                      order = c("coordinate")) 

krige_values_test <- GpGp::predictions(fit = gpfit, 
                      locs_pred = data_test[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                      X_pred = rep(1,nrow(data_test)))
```

```{r}
cat('Test rMSE: with kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median), predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost, design_matrix_test) + krige_values_test))
cat('\n')
cat('Test rMSE: No kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median), predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost, design_matrix_test)))
cat('\n')

cat('Training rMSE: with kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data$concentration_plus_median), predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost, design_matrix) + krige_values))
cat('\n')
cat('Training rMSE: No kriging: ')
cat(Metrics::rmse(log(data$concentration_plus_median), predict(OR_mean_model_xgboost, design_matrix)))
cat('\n')
```

##### Covariate Balancing Weight

```{r}
weights_cbps <- weightit(logWellDepth ~ StaticLevel+WellUse+crop_type_combine+drainagecl+precipitation+cafolog, 
                         data = data, method = "cbps")
```

#### Compute OR smoothers: RKHS 


```{r}
boundary_tol <- 0
depth_range <- c(min(data$logWellDepth) - boundary_tol, max(data$logWellDepth) + boundary_tol)
depth_range
```

```{r}
# xgboost use the design matrix
cf_predictions_RKHS <- precompute_cf_predictions(data_obs = design_matrix, 
                              fit_outcome = svm_auto,
                              treatment_range = depth_range,
                              treatment_step = 0.02,
                              treatment_name = "logWellDepth")
```

```{r}
cf_predictions_RKHS <- isotomic_correction(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                          pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix)
```

```{r}
smoothers_RKHS <- make_prediction_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                            # smooth_method = "gam",
                                            smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                            # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                            spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
cumint_smoothers_RKHS <- make_cumint_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                         # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                         # smooth_method = "gam",
                                         smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                         spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
# xgboost use the design matrix
cf_predictions_test_RKHS <- precompute_cf_predictions(data_obs = design_matrix_test, 
                              fit_outcome = svm_auto,
                              treatment_range = depth_range,
                              treatment_step = 0.02,
                              treatment_name = "logWellDepth")
```

```{r}
cf_predictions_test_RKHS <- isotomic_correction(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                          pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix)
```

```{r}
smoothers_test_RKHS <- make_prediction_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                            smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                            spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
cumint_smoothers_test_RKHS <- make_cumint_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                               pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                               # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                               smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                               spar = NULL)
```

##### Visualizetion of OR Smoothers

```{r}
i <- 11
# when i = 20, it decrease first then increase, 
plot(seq(0,8,length.out = 100), sapply(seq(0,8,length.out = 100), function(knot) {smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'Smooth')
plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, sapply(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, function(knot) {smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'Smoothers RKHS')

plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, sapply(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, function(knot) {cumint_smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'cumint Smoothers RKHS')

plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,  cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix[,i], main = 'Non-smooth original')
```

```{r}
mean(sapply(1:nrow(data), function(i){smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[1])>= log(5)}))
mean(sapply(1:nrow(data), function(i){smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[1]) + krige_values_RKHS[i] >= log(5)}))
```

```{r}
mean(data$logconcentration_plus_median >= log(5))
mean(data$logconcentration_plus_median >= log(2))
```

#### Compute OR smoothers: XGBoost

This haven't been settled yet. We will take care of it later.
```{r}
boundary_tol <- 0
depth_range <- c(min(data$logWellDepth) - boundary_tol, max(data$logWellDepth) + boundary_tol)
depth_range
```

```{r}
# xgboost use the design matrix
cf_predictions_RKHS <- precompute_cf_predictions(data_obs = design_matrix, 
                              fit_outcome = svm_auto,
                              treatment_range = depth_range,
                              treatment_step = 0.02,
                              treatment_name = "logWellDepth")
```

```{r}
cf_predictions_RKHS <- isotomic_correction(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                          pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix)
```

```{r}
smoothers_RKHS <- make_prediction_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                            # smooth_method = "gam",
                                            smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                            # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                            spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
cumint_smoothers_RKHS <- make_cumint_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                         # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                         # smooth_method = "gam",
                                         smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                         spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
# xgboost use the design matrix
cf_predictions_test_RKHS <- precompute_cf_predictions(data_obs = design_matrix_test, 
                              fit_outcome = svm_auto,
                              treatment_range = depth_range,
                              treatment_step = 0.02,
                              treatment_name = "logWellDepth")
```

```{r}
cf_predictions_test_RKHS <- isotomic_correction(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                          pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix)
```

```{r}
smoothers_test_RKHS <- make_prediction_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                            pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                            smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                            spar = NULL)
```

```{r}
cumint_smoothers_test_RKHS <- make_cumint_smoothers(treatment_grid = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$treatment_values,
                                               pred_matrix_cf = cf_predictions_test_RKHS$pred_matrix,
                                               # smooth_method = "splinefun",
                                               smooth_method = "smooth.spline",
                                               spar = NULL)
```

##### Visualizetion of OR Smoothers

```{r}
i <- 11
# when i = 20, it decrease first then increase, 
plot(seq(0,8,length.out = 100), sapply(seq(0,8,length.out = 100), function(knot) {smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'Smooth')
plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, sapply(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, function(knot) {smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'Smoothers RKHS')

plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, sapply(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values, function(knot) {cumint_smoothers_RKHS[[i]](knot)}), main = 'cumint Smoothers RKHS')

plot(cf_predictions_RKHS$treatment_values,  cf_predictions_RKHS$pred_matrix[,i], main = 'Non-smooth original')
```

```{r}
mean(sapply(1:nrow(data), function(i){smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[1])>= log(5)}))
mean(sapply(1:nrow(data), function(i){smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[1]) + krige_values_RKHS[i] >= log(5)}))
```

```{r}
mean(data$logconcentration_plus_median >= log(5))
mean(data$logconcentration_plus_median >= log(2))
```


#### Indirect Method
##### Indirect Method OR

```{r}
threshold_val = log(5)
```

```{r}
indirect_policy_RKHS <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS, spatial = TRUE)
indirect_policy_test_RKHS <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = krige_values_test_RKHS, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
Metrics::accuracy(log(data$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val, indirect_policy_RKHS > data$logWellDepth)
yardstick::mcc_vec(truth = as.factor(log(data$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val), estimate = as.factor( indirect_policy_RKHS > data$logWellDepth))
```

```{r}
Metrics::accuracy(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val, indirect_policy_test_RKHS > data_test$logWellDepth)
yardstick::mcc_vec(truth = as.factor(log(data_test$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val), estimate = as.factor( indirect_policy_test_RKHS > data_test$logWellDepth))
```

##### Indirect Method DB

```{r}
resids <- data$logconcentration_plus_median - predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix) - krige_values_RKHS
scaled_resids <- resids * weights_cbps$weights
kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
```

```{r}
indirect_policy_DB <- sapply(1:nrow(data), function(i) {
  # if lower bound can alreay be lower than the threshold (satisfy the ES), we have to use lower bound
  if(smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[1]) + krige_values_RKHS[i] + scaled_resids[i] * gaussian_kernel(depth_range[1], data$logWellDepth[i], sigma = kernel_bw) <= threshold_val) return(depth_range[1])
  # if even upper bound can not be lower than the threshold, we have to use upper bound
  if(smoothers_RKHS[[i]](depth_range[2]) + krige_values_RKHS[i] + scaled_resids[i] * gaussian_kernel(depth_range[2], data$logWellDepth[i], sigma = kernel_bw)> threshold_val) return(depth_range[2])
  res <- uniroot(f = function(t) {smoothers_RKHS[[i]](t) + krige_values_RKHS[i] + scaled_resids[i] * gaussian_kernel(t, data$logWellDepth[i], sigma = kernel_bw)- threshold_val},
        lower = depth_range[1],
        upper = depth_range[2])
  return(res$root)
})
```

Note that the result might be local minimum

```{r}
Metrics::accuracy(log(data$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val, indirect_policy_DB> data$logWellDepth)
yardstick::mcc_vec(truth = as.factor(log(data$concentration_plus_median) > threshold_val), estimate = as.factor( indirect_policy_DB > data$logWellDepth))
```


#### A small test: 20 x 20 kernel grand matrix

```{r}
# we use the following one for now
GPS_covariate_names <- c("StaticLevel","WellUse","crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
kernel_design_matrix <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_RKHS))[,-1]
kernel_design_matrix_test <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data_test[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_test_RKHS))[,-1]

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

Sub-sampling 20 samples

```{r}
set.seed(1998)
sub_indices <- sample(nrow(kernel_design_matrix), 20)
kernel_design_matrix_small <- kernel_design_matrix[sub_indices,]
indirect_policy_RKHS_small <- indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]
```

Draw the loss function for every individual and see if the indirect method is the minimum

```{r}
resids <- data$logconcentration_plus_median - predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix) - krige_values_RKHS
scaled_resids <- resids * weights_cbps$weights
# plot(scaled_resids)
kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
```

##### Visualize individual loss with policy

Narrow depth range loss

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "or")}), cex = 0.1)
  abline(v = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], col = 'red')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
}
```

Wide depth range loss

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 10, depth_range[2] + 10, length.out = 200)

for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 30,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "or")}), cex = 0.1)
  abline(v = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], col = 'red')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
}
```


##### Individually optimize loss

optimize every single loss function individually. We can see that when initial is very far from the boundary, the gradient will be too small. 

For the individual case, we can see using a very large clip_epsilon can ease the problem.

```{r}

for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  direct_trt <- optim(par = 30,
                      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth,
                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon ,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "or",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 3))
  cat(direct_trt$par,',', indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
}

```

##### Optimize sum of individual loss with identity kernel

Then, we optimize the sum of the loss function every single loss function.

To get the initial value, we build kernel matrix

```{r}
m <- 0
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_small))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_small)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,20), k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB_small, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```

When it has a bad initial treatment, i.e., far from the boundary, it is very hard to get to the point 

```{r}
direct_optim_trt <- optim(par = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet,
      fn = compute_total_loss_sum_smooth,
      # gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth,
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 200, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

I change the loss function outside the boundary, however, the result when I specify gr is slightly not optimal because it is non-smooth at boundary.
Then, we try quadratic loss, and it is better.

```{r}
direct_optim_trt <- optim(par = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet,
      fn = compute_total_loss_sum_smooth,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth, # I think its problem is at the boundary
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 200, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, direct_optim_trt$par, main = 'un-winsorized')
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, pmax(pmin(direct_optim_trt$par, depth_range[2]),depth_range[1]), main = 'winsorized')
```

calculate the loss function

```{r}
compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = as.numeric(direct_optim_trt$par),
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_RKHS_small,
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")
```


##### Optimize sum of individual loss with non-identity kernel: small dataset

```{r}
m <- -1
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_small))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_small)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,20), k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB_small, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
hist(k_matrix)
```

The result highly depends on the form of the kernel matrix, when the kernel matrix is very smooth, it cannot perfectly fit the indirect method.

```{r}
kernel_optim_trt <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet,
      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      K = k_matrix,
      # K = cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)),
      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
      outcome_resid = rep(0,20),
      propensity_est = rep(1,20),
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 500, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)
# plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)) %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)

```

```{r}

compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params =  kernel_optim_trt$par,
      # K = k_matrix, 
      K = cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)),
      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
      outcome_resid = rep(0,20),
      propensity_est = rep(1,20),
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_RKHS_small,
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")
```



##### Optimize sum of individual loss with identity kernel: whole dataset

```{r}
m <- 0
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
hist(k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```


```{r}
direct_optim_trt <- optim(# par = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet,
                          par = cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)),diag(nrow(k_matrix))) %*% params_initial_glmnet - 17,
      fn = compute_total_loss_sum_smooth,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth,
      # subject_idx = sub_indices,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(data),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(factr = 1e4, maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = as.numeric(direct_optim_trt$par),
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(k_matrix),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_RKHS,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(k_matrix),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS, as.numeric(direct_optim_trt$par))
```
Some of it are not able to optimize, that's check their loss function

```{r}
which.max(indirect_policy_RKHS - as.numeric(direct_optim_trt$par))
which.min(indirect_policy_RKHS - as.numeric(direct_optim_trt$par))
```

Then, we get the initial value

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 0.2, depth_range[2] + 0.2, length.out = 200)
plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = 1421,
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 30,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "or")}), cex = 0.1)
abline(v = indirect_policy_RKHS[1421], col = 'red')
abline(v = direct_optim_trt$par[1421], col = 'green')
abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("Indirect Policy (RKHS)", "Direct Optim (Treatment)"),
       col = c("red", "green"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)                           # Optional: Make the lines in the legend a bit thicker for visibility

print(compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_RKHS[1421],
                                                                          subject_idx = 1421,
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 30,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "or") - compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = direct_optim_trt$par[1421],
                                                                          subject_idx = 1421,
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 30,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "or"))
```


##### Optimize sum of individual loss with non-identity kernel: whole dataset

```{r}
m <- - 1.5
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)

k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

hist(k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```
Evaluate the difference between intial point and the the policy that minimized the loss function.

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS, indirect_policy_DB)
plot(indirect_policy_DB, k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```
For OR loss function there is no need to do DC algorithm because the loss function is convex

```{r}
kernel_optim_trt <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet,
      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      K = k_matrix,
      # K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = rep(0,nrow(k_matrix)),
      propensity_est = rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)),
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS, k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)
# plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)) %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)
plot(k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet, k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt$par, main = 'comprison of fitted policy before and after')
plot(params_initial_glmnet, kernel_optim_trt$par, main = 'comprison of parameters before and after')
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params =  kernel_optim_trt$par,
      K = k_matrix,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = rep(0,nrow(k_matrix)),
      propensity_est = rep(0,nrow(k_matrix)),
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_RKHS,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(k_matrix),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "or")
```

###### evaluate 

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = indirect_policy_RKHS, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt$par, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix_test %*% kernel_optim_trt$par, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```






#### A small test/ A complete Test: 20 x 20 kernel grand matrix/ whole grand matrix: DB Method

Now we do the above procedure for DB method, to note that, the loss function of DB method might not be convex due to the augmented term.

```{r}
# we use the following one for now
GPS_covariate_names <- c("StaticLevel","WellUse","crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
kernel_design_matrix <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_RKHS))[,-1]
kernel_design_matrix_test <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data_test[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_test_RKHS))[,-1]

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

sub-sampling 20 samples, here we might draw samples where the DB policy is very different from the OR indirect policy

```{r}
set.seed(1998)
# sub_indices <- sample(nrow(kernel_design_matrix), 20)
# sub_indices <- sample(which(abs(indirect_policy_DB - indirect_policy_RKHS) >= 0.4), 20)
sub_indices <- sample(which(abs(indirect_policy_DB - indirect_policy_RKHS) >= 0.5), 20)
kernel_design_matrix_small <- kernel_design_matrix[sub_indices,]
indirect_policy_RKHS_small <- indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]
indirect_policy_DB_small <- indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]
```

##### Visualize individual loss with policy

Draw the DB loss function for every individual and see if the indirect method is the minimum

```{r}
resids <- data$logconcentration_plus_median - predict(svm_auto, newdata = design_matrix) - krige_values_RKHS
scaled_resids <- resids * weights_cbps$weights
# plot(scaled_resids)
kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
```

Narrow depth range loss.

The looks like the problem is every DB indirect method is a local minimum of the loss function

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.1)
  abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col = 'red')
  abline(v = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], col = 'green')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("Indirect Policy (DB)", "Indirect Policy (RKHS)"),
       col = c("red", "green"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)                           # Optional: Make the lines in the legend a bit thicker for visibility

}
```

For i = 6, the DB result is false? I think this is because when we are finding the indirect method, we are not minimizing the loss function, we are trying to find the point where the adjusted outcome regression is equal to threshold, which might actually be local minimum. 

in i=6, there are actually two of these



#### non-DC algorithm for DB Method
##### Individually optimize DB loss: non-DC algorithm: small dataset

optimize every single loss function individually. We can see that when initial is very far from the boundary, the gradient will be too small. 

For the individual case, we can see using a very large clip_epsilon can ease the problem.

```{r}
direct_individual_small <- c()
for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  direct_trt <- optim(# par = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],
                      # par = mean(depth_range),
                      # par = depth_range[1],
                      par = depth_range[2],
                      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth,
                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon ,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
  cat('optim trt: ', direct_trt$par,'\n\n')
  direct_individual_small <- c(direct_individual_small, direct_trt$par)
  cat('indirect RKHS: ',indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
  cat('indirect DB: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
}

```

```{r}
direct_individual_small
cat('\n')
indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]
cat('\n')
indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]
```

It seems like let middle to be the starting point is the best, letting RKHS to be the starting point might be good.

```{r}
which(abs(direct_individual_small - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)
```


```{r}
which(abs(direct_individual_small - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)

which(abs(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices] - indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]) > 0.01)
```

Use DB loss with direct quasi-Newton can improve some but many still cannot reach the DB indirect policy, i.e., the minimum of DB loss function.

##### Individually optimize DB loss: non-DC algorithm: whole dataset

optimize every single loss function individually. We can see that when initial is very far from the boundary, the gradient will be too small. 

For the individual case, we can see using a very large clip_epsilon can ease the problem.

We have a problem when we do DC RKHS identity grand matrix for whole dataset, so we go back to the beginning and check each step for whole dataset.
For the individual value, we will try the one we use in 'DC RKHS identity grand matrix for whole dataset'.

```{r}
initial_policy <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet

direct_individual <- c()
for(i in 1:nrow(data)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  direct_trt <- optim(# par = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],
                      # par = mean(depth_range),
                      # par = depth_range[1],
                      par = initial_policy[i],
                      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth,
                      subject_idx = i,
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon ,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
  # cat('optim trt: ', direct_trt$par,'\n\n')
  direct_individual <- c(direct_individual, direct_trt$par)
  # cat('indirect RKHS: ',indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
  # cat('indirect DB: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
}

```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB, direct_individual)
plot(indirect_policy_RKHS, direct_individual)
```


It seems like let middle to be the starting point is the best, letting RKHS to be the starting point might be good.

```{r}
which(abs(direct_individual - indirect_policy_DB) > 0.1)
which(abs(direct_individual - indirect_policy_DB) > 1)
which(abs(direct_individual - indirect_policy_DB) > 2)
which(abs(direct_individual - indirect_policy_DB) > 2.2)
```
```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

for(i in which(abs(direct_individual - indirect_policy_DB) > 2)){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = i,
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.1)
  abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[i], col = 'red')
  abline(v = direct_individual[i], col = 'green')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("Indirect Policy (DB)", "Direct Individual"),
       col = c("red", "green"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)                           # Optional: Make the lines in the legend a bit thicker for visibility

}
```

I think the direct individual actually looks better than indirect policy DB. Let's check its metrics on training data

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = direct_individual, threshold_val = log(5))
```

Yes, it is. The direct individual actually looks better than indirect policy DB, it stucks less in the local minimum.

##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: non-DC algorithm, identity-kernel, small dataset

```{r}
m <- 0
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_small))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_small)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices], alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices], k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
plot(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices], cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)),diag(nrow(k_matrix))) %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
kernel_optim_trt_DB_small <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet,
      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      # K = k_matrix,
      K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)),diag(nrow(k_matrix))),
      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
      outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices], cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)),diag(nrow(k_matrix))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_small$par)
# plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)) %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)
# plot(cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet, cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, main = 'comprison of fitted policy before and after')
# plot(params_initial_glmnet, kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, main = 'comprison of parameters before and after')
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params =  kernel_optim_trt_DB$par,
      # K = k_matrix,
      K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_DB,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(k_matrix),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, threshold_val = log(5))
```

##### Sum optimize DB loss: non-DC algorithm: whole dataset

optimize every single loss function individually. We can see that when initial is very far from the boundary, the gradient will be too small. 

For the individual case, we can see using a very large clip_epsilon can ease the problem.

We have a problem when we do DC RKHS identity grand matrix for whole dataset, so we go back to the beginning and check each step for whole dataset.
For the individual value, we will try the one we use in 'DC RKHS identity grand matrix for whole dataset'.

```{r}
initial_policy <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet

direct_sum_optim <- optim(par = initial_policy,
      fn = compute_total_loss_sum_smooth,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(data),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon ,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db",
      method = "L-BFGS-B", 
      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB, direct_sum_optim$par)
```

The following comments are from individually loss minimization, not sum:

It seems like let middle to be the starting point is the best, letting RKHS to be the starting point might be good.
I think the direct individual actually looks better than indirect policy DB. Let's check its metrics on training data
Yes, it is. The direct individual actually looks better than indirect policy DB, it stucks less in the local minimum.

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = direct_sum_optim$par, threshold_val = log(5))
```


##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: non-DC algorithm, identity-kernel, whole dataset

The algorithm fails but I think the implementation code does not have the problem, I guess it is because the landscape of the loss is no longer convex, making it harder to optimize. I guess that might be the reason we use DCA? Or using a better initial point might help?

```{r}
m <- 0
m <- -2
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
hist(k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB, k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
plot(indirect_policy_DB, cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)),diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
# let's see if can be resolved if using a different initial point, we change the m to make the grand matrix generating initial point more similar to identity kernel
kernel_optim_trt_DB <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet, 
                             # par = initial_policy,
      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      # K = k_matrix,
      K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
      # K = diag(nrow(data)),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB, cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par)
# plot(indirect_policy_RKHS_small, cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)) %*% kernel_optim_trt$par)
plot(cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet, cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, main = 'comprison of fitted policy before and after')
plot(params_initial_glmnet, kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, main = 'comprison of parameters before and after')
```

```{r}
# Test if they are equal: eqal
compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params = params_initial_glmnet, 
                               K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
                               T = data$logWellDepth,
                               krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                              outcome_resid = resids,
                              propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                              lambda = 0, 
                              smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
                              cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                              trt_bounds = depth_range,
                              threshold_val = log(5), 
                              kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                              clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                               surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                              loss_type = "db")
compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet ,
                              subject_idx = 1:nrow(data),
                              T = data$logWellDepth,
                              krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                              outcome_resid = resids,
                              propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                              cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                              smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
                              trt_bounds = depth_range,
                              threshold_val = log(5), 
                              kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                              clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                              surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                              loss_type = "db")
```

```{r}
# test if they are equal
d_1 <- d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                 K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
                                 T = data$logWellDepth,
                                 krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                 outcome_resid = resids,
                                 propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                 lambda = 0, 
                                 smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
                                 cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                 trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                 threshold_val = log(5), 
                                 kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                 clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                 surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                 loss_type = "db")
d_2 <- d_compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet ,
                              subject_idx = 1:nrow(data),
                              T = data$logWellDepth,
                              krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                              outcome_resid = resids,
                              propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                              cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                              smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
                              trt_bounds = depth_range,
                              threshold_val = log(5), 
                              kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                              clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                              surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                              loss_type = "db")
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params =  kernel_optim_trt_DB$par,
      # K = k_matrix,
      K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      lambda = 0, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_DB,
      subject_idx = 1:nrow(k_matrix),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, threshold_val = log(5))
```

##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: non-DC algorithm, non-identity-kernel, small dataset
##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: non-DC algorithm, non-identity-kernel, whole dataset

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = indirect_policy_DB, threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, policy = direct_individual, threshold_val = log(5))
```

We choose direct_individual to get the initial value of coefficient

```{r}
m <- - 1.5
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)

k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

hist(k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(direct_individual, k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```


```{r}
# let's see if can be resolved if using a different initial point, we change the m to make the grand matrix generating initial point more similar to identity kernel
kernel_optim_trt_DB <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet, 
      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
      K = k_matrix,
      # K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))),
      # K = diag(nrow(data)),
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      lambda = 1, 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS, 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db",
      method = "L-BFGS-B",
      control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) # it seems like the default is 100 iterations
      # control = list(trace = 3))
```

```{r}
plot(params_initial_glmnet, kernel_optim_trt_DB$par)
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = direct_individual, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix_test %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB$par, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```







#### DC algorithm for DB Method

##### preparation

```{r}
# we use the following one for now
GPS_covariate_names <- c("StaticLevel","WellUse","crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
kernel_design_matrix <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_RKHS))[,-1]
kernel_design_matrix_test <- model.matrix(~ . , data = cbind(data_test[, GPS_covariate_names], U = krige_values_test_RKHS))[,-1]

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

sub-sampling 20 samples, here we might draw samples where the DB policy is very different from the OR indirect policy

```{r}
set.seed(1998)
# sub_indices <- sample(nrow(kernel_design_matrix), 20)
# sub_indices <- sample(which(abs(indirect_policy_DB - indirect_policy_RKHS) >= 0.4), 20)
sub_indices <- sample(which(abs(indirect_policy_DB - indirect_policy_RKHS) >= 0.5), 20)
kernel_design_matrix_small <- kernel_design_matrix[sub_indices,]
indirect_policy_RKHS_small <- indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]
indirect_policy_DB_small <- indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]
```

##### Individually optimize DB loss: DC algorithm, small dataset

We can see that some of it is not able to leave the local minimum of at RKHS indirect policy. Thus, we try DC algorithm

###### Check loss decomposition

First, we plot the loss functions, the result seems reasonable

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 5, depth_range[2] + 5, length.out = 200)
i <- 2 # try it for different sample, i.e., different i
plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}) -
              sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_plus(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 2 * clip_epsilon,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db") - 
                                              compute_total_loss_smooth_minus(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 2 * clip_epsilon,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")} ), cex = 0.3, lwd = 3)  
abline(v = depth_range[1],col = 'blue')
abline(v = depth_range[2],col = 'blue')
              
plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_plus(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 2 * clip_epsilon,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db") - 
                                              compute_total_loss_smooth_minus(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
```
