```{r}
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 30,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}) - sapply(depth_grid, function(t){numDeriv::grad(function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_minus(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 30,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}, x = t)}))
```

###### visualize DC approximation 

we choose different 
```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 0.2, depth_range[2] + 0.2, length.out = 200)
i <- 2 # try it for different sample, i.e., different i
assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[1]
assigned_trt_old <- mean(depth_range)
plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                          smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2, col = 'blue')

lines(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2)
abline(v = assigned_trt_old, col='red')
abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col='green')

```
Why it is decreasing when t is larger than right boundary. Because the clip_epsilon_bar is not large enough, it has to be larger than

Also, we found that initial point is important, there is possibility that DC algorithm would be stuck in local minimum. For example, if the initial point is the minimum of DC approx loss but is not the global minimum of original loss.

###### Implementation of Individually optimize DB loss: DC algorithm

I also think in true applications, the DC algorithm is no faster than regular optimization, for individual. The DC individual is extremely slow, 

For optimizing the the whole together, I am not sure if DC algorithm would be faster

```{r}
kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
direct_individual_DC <- c()

initial_policy <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(data)), diag(nrow(data))) %*% params_initial_glmnet

for(i in 1:nrow(data)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  # assigned_trt_old <- mean(depth_range)
  # assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[1]
  # assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[2]
  max_iter <- 100
  for(k in 1:max_iter) {
    sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                        fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, 
                        gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                        assigned_trt_old = initial_policy[i],
                        subject_idx = i,
                        T = data$logWellDepth,
                        krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                        outcome_resid = resids,
                        propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                        cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                        smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                        trt_bounds = depth_range,
                        threshold_val = log(5), 
                        kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                        clip_epsilon = 1,
                        clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                        surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                        loss_type = "db",
                        method = "L-BFGS-B" )#, 
                        # control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
    
    assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
    
    
    # 3. Check for convergence
    # change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
    # cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
    
    if (change < tol) {
      cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
      assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
      break
    }
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
  }
  # cat('individual_small_DC: ', assigned_trt_old,'\n\n')
  direct_individual_DC <- c(direct_individual_DC, assigned_trt_old)
  # cat('indirect RKHS: ',indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
  # cat('indirect DB: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
}
```

Nothing in it is correct

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices], direct_individual_DC[sub_indices], ylim = c(depth_range[1]-2, depth_range[2]+2))
```
We can see that there are some very extreme value of direct_individual_DC, we will check it 

```{r}
sum(direct_individual_DC>=depth_range[2] + 0.001)
sum(direct_individual_DC<=depth_range[1] - 0.001)
sum(abs(direct_individual_DC - indirect_policy_DB) > 0.1)
```


After we did this we get back to the small dataset, and found that using initial_policy makes the optimization fails for almost every individual. This might because the DC algorithm is way too sensitive to initial value. Or because our loss is not that smooth at boundary point. 


```{r}
which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)

which(abs(direct_individual_small - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)

which(abs(indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices] - indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices]) > 0.01)
```

```{r}
direct_individual_small_DC[which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)]
indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)]]
```


Then, we visualize the loss function where samples DC fails to find the optimum

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

# for(i in which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)){
for(i in which(abs(direct_individual_small - indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices]) > 0.01)){
  cat('loss function of ',i,'-th sample\n')
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.1, main = paste(i,'-th sample'))
  abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col = 'red')
  abline(v = indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], col = 'green')
  abline(v = direct_individual_small_DC[i], col = 'yellow')
  abline(v = mean(depth_range), col = 'purple')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("Indirect Policy (DB)", "Indirect Policy (RKHS)", "Direct (Indiviaul DB DC)","Initial Point"),
       col = c("red", "green","yellow","purple"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)                           # Optional: Make the lines in the legend a bit thicker for visibility

}
```

I think what in common is they stuck in local minimum, let's visualize DC algorithm for these sample step by step. If we increase iteration steps, can this problem will resolved?

It cannot be resolved, actually, DC algorithm is local minimum algorithm and can be stuck on stationary point. It only guarantees that it converges to a critical point. 

Whether or not converging to global optimum highly depends on the starting points. A multi-start approach is suggesting. We can choose multiple starting point, and the choose the converging point with the minimal loss function.

Based on our experiment, using the middle of depth range gives the best result.

```{r}
max_iter <- 50
tol <- 1e-6

kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
depth_range <- c(min(data$logWellDepth), max(data$logWellDepth))


i <- 2

assigned_trt_old <- indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]]

for (k in 1:max_iter) {
    
    # Solve the convex subproblem
    cat(k,'-th iteration of DC algorithm','\n')
    sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                        fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, 
                        # gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                        assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                        subject_idx = train_indices[i],
                        T = data$logWellDepth,
                        krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                        outcome_resid = resids,
                        propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                        cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                        smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                        trt_bounds = depth_range,
                        threshold_val = log(5), 
                        kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                        clip_epsilon = 1,
                        clip_epsilon_bar = 50,
                        surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                        loss_type = "db",
                        method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                        control = list(maxit = 60, trace = 3))
    
    assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
    
    # 3. Check for convergence
    change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
    cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
    
    if (change < tol) {
      cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
      assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
      break
    }
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
}
cat('DC individual result: ', assigned_trt_old, '\n')
cat('indirect DB policy: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], '\n')
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                          outcome_resid = resids,
                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                          loss_type = "db")

compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]],
                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                          outcome_resid = resids,
                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                          loss_type = "db")
```

###### visualize DC approximation step by step

```{r}
max_iter <- 100
tol <- 1e-4

kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
depth_range <- c(min(data$logWellDepth), max(data$logWellDepth))

depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 0.5, depth_range[2] + 0.5, length.out = 200)

i <- 2

assigned_trt_old <- mean(depth_range)
assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[1]

for (k in 1:max_iter) {
    
    # Solve the convex subproblem
    # cat(k,'-th iteration of DC algorithm','\n')
    sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                        fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, 
                        # gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                        assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                        subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                        T = data$logWellDepth,
                        krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                        outcome_resid = resids,
                        propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                        cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                        smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                        trt_bounds = depth_range,
                        threshold_val = log(5), 
                        kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                        clip_epsilon = 1,
                        clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                        surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                        loss_type = "db",
                        method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                        control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
    
    assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
    
    # draw the plot
    plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                          kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2, col = 'blue')

    lines(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2)
    abline(v = assigned_trt, col='red')
    abline(v = assigned_trt_old, col='blue')
    abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col='green')

    legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("sub optimal", "initial policy", "optimal"),
       col = c("red", "blue","green"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)
    
    # 3. Check for convergence
    # change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
    # cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
    
    if (change < tol) {
      cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
      assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
      break
    }
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
}
cat('DC individual result: ', assigned_trt_old, '\n')
cat('indirect DB policy: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], '\n')
cat('indirect RKHS policy: ', indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], '\n')
```

I think this highly depends on the derivative of the original loss function around the initial value, if its slope is small, then the DC algorithm converge very slowly. Also, quadratic function is very slow at the boundary.

For i equal to 6, the DC still work, the DB indirect policy is not correct.


###### check derivative of loss dc approximation

We first visualize one of it

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)
i <- 10 # try it for different sample, i.e., different i

plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                                      assigned_trt_old = mean(depth_range),
                                                                                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                                      outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                                      threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon_bar = 70,
                                                                                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                                      loss_type = "db")}))

plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){numDeriv::grad(function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                                      assigned_trt_old = mean(depth_range),
                                                                                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                                      outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                                      threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon_bar = 70,
                                                                                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                                      loss_type = "db")}, x = t)}))

plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                                      assigned_trt_old = mean(depth_range),
                                                                                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                                      outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                                      threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon_bar = 70,
                                                                                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                                      loss_type = "db")}) - sapply(depth_grid, function(t){numDeriv::grad(function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                                      assigned_trt_old = mean(depth_range),
                                                                                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                                      outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                                      threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                                      kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                                      clip_epsilon_bar = 70,
                                                                                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                                      loss_type = "db")}, x = t)}))
```

Then check the optimization use or not use gr in optim


```{r}
kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
direct_individual_small_DC <- c()

for(i in 1:length(sub_indices)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  assigned_trt_old <- mean(depth_range)
  # assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[1]
  # assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[2]
  max_iter <- 100
  for(k in 1:max_iter) {
    
    # Solve the convex subproblem
    # cat(k,'-th iteration of DC algorithm','\n')
    sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                        fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, 
                        gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                        assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                        subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                        T = data$logWellDepth,
                        krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                        outcome_resid = resids,
                        propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                        cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                        smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                        trt_bounds = depth_range,
                        threshold_val = log(5), 
                        kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                        clip_epsilon = 1,
                        clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                        surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                        loss_type = "db",
                        method = "L-BFGS-B" )#, 
                        # control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
    
    assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
    
    
    # 3. Check for convergence
    # change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
    # cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
    
    if (change < tol) {
      cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
      assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
      break
    }
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
  }
  cat('individual_small_DC: ', assigned_trt_old,'\n\n')
  direct_individual_small_DC <- c(direct_individual_small_DC, assigned_trt_old)
  cat('indirect RKHS: ',indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
  cat('indirect DB: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]],'\n\n')
  
}


```

```{r}
direct_individual_small_DC
```

```{r}
direct_individual_small_DC
```


##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: DC algorithm, small dataset

We compare the individual result and the sum result

```{r}
assigned_trt_old <- rep(mean(depth_range), length(sub_indices))
# assigned_trt_old <- rep(depth_range[1] - 5, length(sub_indices))
# assigned_trt_old <- rep(depth_range[1] + 5, length(sub_indices))
assigned_trt_old <- cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet
max_iter <- 100
for(k in 1:max_iter) {
  
  
  sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                      fn = compute_total_loss_sum_smooth_dc_approx, # for multiple of it, we have to use sum
                      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                      assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                      subject_idx = sub_indices,
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = 1,
                      clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B" , 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 3))
  
  assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
  
  
  # 3. Check for convergence
  change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
  cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
  
  if (change < tol) {
    cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
    break
  }
  assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
}

direct_sum_small_DC <- assigned_trt_old

```

```{r}
cat('direct_sum_small_DC: ', direct_sum_small_DC,'\n\n')
cat('direct_individual_small_DC: ',direct_individual_small_DC ,'\n\n')
cat('indirect_small_DB: ',indirect_policy_DB_small ,'\n\n')
```

```{r}
plot(direct_individual_small_DC, direct_sum_small_DC)
```

Some test and evaluation if the results of direct_sum_small_DC and direct_individual_small_DCdo not match 

```{r}
which(abs(direct_sum_small_DC - direct_individual_small_DC) > 0.1)
which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - indirect_policy_DB_small) > 0.1)
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = direct_sum_small_DC,
                      subject_idx = sub_indices,
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = 1,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db")
compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = direct_individual_small_DC,
                      subject_idx = sub_indices,
                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                      outcome_resid = resids,
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                      clip_epsilon = 1,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db")
```

##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: DC algorithm, identity-kernel, small dataset

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix_small <- kernel_design_matrix[sub_indices,]
m <- 0
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_small))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_small)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,20), k_matrix)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB_small, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB_small, k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
plot(indirect_policy_DB_small, cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```
Check gradient of the kernel

```{r}
d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           params_old = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            # clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            # clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")

numDeriv::grad(func = function(params){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params, 
                                           params_old = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            # clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            # clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")}, x = params_initial_glmnet)
```
We need to select epsilon_bar that is large enough so that it converges fast enough outside the boundary

```{r}
max_iter <- 300
tol <- 1e-4
params_old <- params_initial_glmnet
# params_old <- c(0,cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_initial_glmnet)
# params_old <- c(0, rep(depth_range[1] - 5,20))
# params_old <- c(0, rep(depth_range[2] + 5,20))
for(k in 1:max_iter) {
  sub_result <- optim(par = params_old, 
                      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS, # for multiple of it, we have to use sum
                      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS, # we try the case with/without gr, we found
                      params_old = params_old, 
                      K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                      # K = cbind(rep(0,20),diag(20)),
                      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                      outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                      lambda = 0, 
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                      # clip_epsilon = 1,
                      # clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                      clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B" , 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
  
  params <- sub_result$par
  
  
  # 3. Check for convergence
  change <- sqrt(sum((params_old - params)^2))
  cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
  
  if (change < tol) {
    cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
    params_old <- params
    break
  }
  params_old <- params
}
```


The following are past comments that are not correct:
It suddenly worked, I change the delta (boundary small value) in d_compute_total_loss_smooth_plus in d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx to zero, but keep the d_compute_total_loss_smooth_minus to 0.001. However, this setting seems to be problematic in individual optimization?

Actually, I don't know what is going on very precisely. But it is something around the boundary

we found that not specifying gr can optimize successfully. That means the d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS must be problematic at some point

compute the value of loss function

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS(params =  params_old,
      # K = k_matrix,
      K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
      outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
      lambda = 0, 
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")

compute_total_loss_sum_smooth(assigned_trt = indirect_policy_DB_small,
      subject_idx = sub_indices,
      T = data$logWellDepth,
      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
      outcome_resid = resids,
      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
      trt_bounds = depth_range,
      threshold_val = log(5), 
      kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
      loss_type = "db")
```


###### Diagnosis

Check the derivative

```{r}
d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params_old, 
                                           params_old = params_old, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")

grad(func = function(params){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params, 
                                           params_old = params_old, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")}, x = params_old)
```
The derivative is clearly not correct. Let's figure out why.

First, we get the assigned treatment under the parameter
```{r}
K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20))
assigned_trt_old <- as.vector(K %*% params_old)
assigned_trt_old
```

Then, we figure out which treatment has wrong derivative 

```{r}
d_total_loss_dc_approx <- d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                                            assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                                            subject_idx = 1:20,
                                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                                            clip_epsilon = 1,
                                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                            loss_type = "db")
d_total_loss_dc_approx
as.numeric((t(K) %*% d_total_loss_dc_approx))
```
```{r}
d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv <- grad(func = function(trt){compute_total_loss_sum_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = trt,
                                                              assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                                              subject_idx = 1:20,
                                                              T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                                              krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                                              outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                                              propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                                              cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                                              smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                                              trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                              threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                              kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                                              clip_epsilon = 1,
                                                              clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                                              surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                              loss_type = "db")}, x = assigned_trt_old)
d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv
```

```{r}
d_total_loss_dc_approx
cat('\n')
d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv
cat('\n')
d_total_loss_dc_approx - d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv
cat('\n')
assigned_trt_old
```
We found the second derivative is problematic where the assigned trt is 3.400917,slightly less than the lower bound

```{r}
for(i in which(abs(d_total_loss_dc_approx - d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv)>=0.1)){
  cat(i,'-th sample\n')
  cat('The assigned treatment is: ', assigned_trt_old[i],'\n')
  cat('Distance to left boundary: ', assigned_trt_old[i] - trt_bounds[1],'\n')
  cat('Distance to right boundary: ', assigned_trt_old[i] - trt_bounds[2],'\n')
  # print(assigned_trt_old[i] - trt_bounds[1])
  # print(trt_bounds[2] - assigned_trt_old[i] )
  cat('numDeriv: ', d_total_loss_dc_approx_numDeriv[i],'\n')
  cat('gr: ', d_total_loss_dc_approx[i],'\n')
}
```
```{r}
i <- 4

```


###### Visualize result

```{r}
plot(direct_individual_small_DC,  cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_old)
abline(0,1,col='red')
```

```{r}
which(abs(indirect_policy_DB_small - cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_old)>0.1)
which(abs(direct_individual_small_DC - cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_old)>0.1)
which(abs(direct_sum_small_DC - cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_old)>0.1)
```

After the visualization, I think the result is overall good

```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

for(i in c(2,6,10,11)){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.1)
  abline(v = indirect_policy_DB_small[i], col = 'red')
  abline(v = (cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)) %*% params_old)[i], col = 'green')
  abline(v = direct_individual_small_DC[i], col = 'yellow')
  abline(v = depth_range[1], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  abline(v = depth_range[2], col = 'blue',lty = 2)
  legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("Indirect Policy (DB)", "Direct Policy (Identity Kernel DC)", "Individual DC"),
       col = c("red", "green", "yellow"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)                           # Optional: Make the lines in the legend a bit thicker for visibility

}
```



##### Optimize Sum of DB loss: DC algorithm, non-identity-kernel, small dataset

###### Implementation

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix_small <- kernel_design_matrix[sub_indices,]
m <- 0
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_small))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_small)
max(k_matrix[row(k_matrix)!=col(k_matrix)])
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,20), k_matrix)
hist(k_matrix)
```

```{r}
# we can change y below to change the initial value
# initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_DB_small, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
# initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = indirect_policy_RKHS_small, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
# initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = rep(mean(depth_range), 20), alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = rep(depth_range[1] - 2, 20), alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
# initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = rep(depth_range[2] + 2, 20), alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
plot(indirect_policy_DB_small, k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```
Check gradient of the kernel

```{r}
d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           params_old = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            # clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            # clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")

numDeriv::grad(func = function(params){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS(params = params, 
                                           params_old = params_initial_glmnet, 
                                           K = cbind(rep(1,20),diag(20)),
                                            T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                            lambda = 0, 
                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                            kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                            # clip_epsilon = 1,
                                            # clip_epsilon_bar = 100,
                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                            loss_type = "db")}, x = params_initial_glmnet)
```
We need to select epsilon_bar that is large enough so that it converges fast enough outside the boundary

```{r}
max_iter <- 300
tol <- 1e-4

params_old <- params_initial_glmnet
for(k in 1:max_iter) {
  sub_result <- optim(par = params_old, 
                      fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS, # for multiple of it, we have to use sum
                      gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx_RKHS, # we try the case with/without gr, we found
                      params_old = params_old, 
                      K = k_matrix,
                      # K = cbind(rep(1,20), diag(20)),
                      T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                      outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                      lambda = 0, 
                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                      threshold_val = log(5), 
                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                      # clip_epsilon = 1,
                      # clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                      clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                      loss_type = "db",
                      method = "L-BFGS-B" , 
                      control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
  
  params <- sub_result$par
  
  
  # 3. Check for convergence
  change <- sqrt(sum((params_old - params)^2))
  cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
  
  if (change < tol) {
    cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
    params_old <- params
    break
  }
  params_old <- params
}
```


###### Diagnosis

We can see

We can only consider the first step of DC algorithm

```{r}
as.numeric(k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet)
```

```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = as.numeric(k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet),
                                    assigned_trt_old = as.numeric(k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet),
                                    subject_idx = 1:20,
                                    T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                    krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                    outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                    cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                    smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                    trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                    threshold_val = log(5), 
                                    kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                    clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                    loss_type = "db")
```

we can see slightly increase the treatment can decrease the loss function

```{r}

compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = rep(1.49,20),
                                    assigned_trt_old = as.numeric(k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet),
                                    subject_idx = 1:20,
                                    T = data$logWellDepth[sub_indices],
                                    krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                    outcome_resid = resids[sub_indices],
                                    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights[sub_indices],
                                    cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices], 
                                    smoothers = smoothers_RKHS[sub_indices],
                                    trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                    threshold_val = log(5), 
                                    kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                                    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                    clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                    loss_type = "db")
```


```{r}
temp_optim <- optim(par = 1.401197,
                    fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx,
                    assigned_trt_old = 1.401197,
                    subject_idx = i,
                    T = data$logWellDepth,
                    krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                    outcome_resid = resids,
                    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                    cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                    smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                    trt_bounds = depth_range,
                    threshold_val = log(5), 
                    kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                    clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                    loss_type = "db")
temp_optim$par
```


```{r}
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 3, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)
for(i in sub_indices){
  plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            assigned_trt_old = 1.401197,
                                                                            subject_idx = i,
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                                                                            smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon * 20,
                                                                            clip_epsilon_bar = 10 * clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.1)
}
```

###### visualize DC approximation step by step

I write the analysis of this in the note, currently, we haven't had a good idea to solve it. 

```{r}
max_iter <- 10
tol <- 1e-5

kernel_bw <- 1.06 * sd(data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data)^(-1/5)
depth_range <- c(min(data$logWellDepth), max(data$logWellDepth))

depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)

i <- 20

# assigned_trt_old <- mean(depth_range)
# assigned_trt_old <- depth_range[1]
assigned_trt_old <- initial_policy[sub_indices[i]]
assigned_trt_old <- 10
for (k in 1:max_iter) {
    
    # Solve the convex subproblem
    # cat(k,'-th iteration of DC algorithm','\n')
    sub_result <- optim(par = assigned_trt_old, 
                        fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, 
                        gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx, # we try the case with/without gr
                        assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                        subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                        T = data$logWellDepth,
                        krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                        outcome_resid = resids,
                        propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                        cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS, 
                        smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                        trt_bounds = depth_range,
                        threshold_val = log(5), 
                        kernel_bw= kernel_bw, 
                        clip_epsilon = 1,
                        clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                        surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                        loss_type = "db",
                        method = "L-BFGS-B", 
                        control = list(maxit = 100, trace = 1))
    
    assigned_trt <- sub_result$par
    
    # draw the plot
    depth_grid <- seq(min(depth_range[1], assigned_trt_old - 0.5), max(depth_range[2] , assigned_trt_old + 0.5), length.out = 200)
    plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                          kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2, col = 'blue', main = 'middle step')

    lines(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                            subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                            T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                            krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                            outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                            propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                            cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                            trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                            threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                            kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                            clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                            surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                            loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2)
    abline(v = assigned_trt, col='red')
    abline(v = assigned_trt_old, col='blue')
    abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col='green')

    legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
       legend = c("sub optimal", "initial policy", "optimal"),
       col = c("red", "blue","green"),
       lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
       lwd = 2)
    
    # 3. Check for convergence
    change <- sqrt(sum((assigned_trt_old - assigned_trt)^2))
    cat(sprintf("Iter: %d, Change: %f, Objective: %f\n", k, change, sub_result$value))
    
    if (change < tol) {
      cat("--- DC Algorithm Converged ---\n")
      assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
      break
    }
    assigned_trt_old <- assigned_trt
}

cat('DC individual result: ', assigned_trt_old, '\n')
cat('indirect DB policy: ', indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], '\n')
cat('indirect RKHS policy: ', indirect_policy_RKHS[sub_indices[i]], '\n')
depth_grid <- seq(depth_range[1] - 2, depth_range[2] + 2, length.out = 200)
plot(depth_grid, sapply(depth_grid, function(t){compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = t,
                                                                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                                                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                                                                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                                                          outcome_resid = resids,
                                                                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                                                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                                                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5),
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                                                                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                                                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                                                          loss_type = "db")}), cex = 0.2, main = 'final')
abline(v = assigned_trt_old, col='blue')
abline(v = indirect_policy_DB[sub_indices[i]], col='green')

legend("topright",                       # You can choose a different position (e.g., "topleft", "bottomright")
   legend = c("sub optimal", "DB optimal"),
   col = c("blue","green"),
   lty = 1,                            # Use lty=1 for solid lines
   lwd = 2)
```
The recent failure of optimization is because I revise the derivative of loss_minus, but not loss_plus, that will make the gr at (right_boundary + small_value) too large, even it is the right direction, it is hard to find a step size that is small enough to make loss_dc_approx decrease.


```{r}
# test the gradient of loss_minus at boundary
compute_total_loss_smooth_plus(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                T = data$logWellDepth,
                                krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                outcome_resid = resids,
                                propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                threshold_val = log(5),
                                kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                loss_type = "db") 

d_compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                      assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                      subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                      T = data$logWellDepth,
                                      krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                      outcome_resid = resids,
                                      propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                      smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                      cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                      trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                      threshold_val = log(5),
                                      kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                      clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                      clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                      surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                      loss_type = "db")
d_compute_total_loss_smooth_plus(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                T = data$logWellDepth,
                                krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                outcome_resid = resids,
                                propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                threshold_val = log(5),
                                kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                loss_type = "db") 

d_compute_total_loss_smooth_minus(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                T = data$logWellDepth,
                                krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                outcome_resid = resids,
                                propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                threshold_val = log(5),
                                kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                loss_type = "db") 

d_compute_total_loss_smooth(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old - 0.1,
                          subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                          T = data$logWellDepth,
                          krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                          outcome_resid = resids,
                          propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                          cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                          smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                          trt_bounds = depth_range,
                          threshold_val = log(5),
                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                          clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                          surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                          loss_type = "db")
```

```{r}
# test dc approx loss function value
compute_total_loss_smooth_dc_approx(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old - 0.2,
                                    assigned_trt_old = assigned_trt_old,
                                    subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                    T = data$logWellDepth,
                                    krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                    outcome_resid = resids,
                                    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                    cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                    smoothers = smoothers_RKHS,
                                    trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                    threshold_val = log(5),
                                    kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                    clip_epsilon_bar = 1000,
                                    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                    loss_type = "db")
```




```{r}
compute_total_loss_smooth_minus(assigned_trt = assigned_trt_old,
                                  subject_idx = sub_indices[i],
                                  T = data$logWellDepth,
                                  krige_adjust = krige_values_RKHS,
                                  outcome_resid = resids,
                                  propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps$weights,
                                  cumint_smoothers = cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
                                  trt_bounds = depth_range,
                                  threshold_val = log(5),
                                  kernel_bw= kernel_bw,
                                  clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
                                  clip_epsilon_bar = 1,
                                  surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
                                  loss_type = "db") 
```

